reviews.review_text:
Good: super fun
Bad: could use some polish by the developer (yes, still after all these years)
Ugly: DLC situation

Recommendation: Buy on discount.

Total War: Warhammer III is what happens when a strategy series fully embraces the fact that subtlety is overrated and that maybe the correct amount of fantasy warfare is “more.”

More monsters. More artillery. More insanity. More factions. More spectacle. More completely unreasonable battlefield decisions that somehow become legendary victories.

And somehow, instead of collapsing under its own excess, it works.

What makes Warhammer 3 special is the sheer confidence of the setting and faction design. Every faction feels like it’s playing a slightly different game. Not fake asymmetry where one faction gets +10% archers and another gets +10% taxes. Actual identity. Kislev feels stubborn and doomed in the best way. Cathay feels orderly and monumental. Chaos feels like the laws of reality filing for bankruptcy.

And then there are the Chaos Dwarfs.

Absolute industrialized evil. Magnificent.

The Chaos Dwarfs are maybe the single best faction Creative Assembly has ever made because every mechanic reinforces exactly who they are. Ruthless, hyper-industrial, cruel beyond reason, and completely unapologetic about it. Your economy runs on slavery, smoke, steel, and the kind of logistical brutality that would make a Victorian coal baron uncomfortable.

And it’s glorious.

The entire campaign feels like managing an evil war machine that constantly demands more labor, more weapons, more fuel, more expansion. Convoys, factories, infernal industry, towering daemon-forges, artillery that looks like it was designed by somebody asking “what if a train hated humanity?” It all fits together perfectly.

The battlefield experience is even better. Few things in strategy gaming compare to watching disciplined lines of infernal guard hold the center while magma cannons and dreadquake mortars turn the horizon into an industrial accident. Then bull centaurs crash into a flank while hobgoblins commit whatever crimes hobgoblins commit when unsupervised.

The scale of Warhammer 3 also deserves credit. Immortal Empires feels less like a campaign map and more like a giant fantasy sandbox where every campaign eventually spirals into mythology. Tiny regional conflicts become continent-wide wars against gods, undead monarchs, rat civilizations, or some screaming demon prince with six axes and emotional issues.

But underneath all the spectacle, the strategy layer is still strong. Diplomacy matters. Economy matters. Army composition matters. Positioning matters. The game succeeds because there’s an actual tactical foundation supporting the absurdity.

Most importantly, Warhammer 3 understands that fantasy strategy games should occasionally feel overwhelming in the best possible way. Not balanced into sterile symmetry. Not reduced to esports minimalism. Grand, ridiculous, thematic warfare where your screen becomes complete chaos and you somehow still pull victory out of the fire.

Hashut approves. And honestly, that’s enough for me.

At it's current state it's really boring and unbalanced.
The AI attacks only unwalled settlements which makes it a cat and mouse chase game, since CA in it's infinite wisdom removed minor settlements walls.
The AI also goes out of it's way to go around your armies, and not attacking them.(Total Chase: Warhammer 3)
The AI's AntiPlayer Bias is outright silly, and makes you run around putting out fires which removes the grand strategy layer or at least reduce it significally.
The Vassal system and Alliance system is broken. 
As soon as you ally or subjucate a faction, they become totally braindead.
The AI cheats is also out of whack!

In it's state i will not recommend the game, it's broken and unbalanced.
I really used to love this series, but it needs alot of fixes AI and campaign wise!

I bought the "Total War: Warhammer III Collection" bundle idiotically believing that it would unlock all of the content for the game. What a fool I was. CREATIVE ASSEMBLY and Games Workshop are greedy. How can you hope for a fair, clear sale from the greedy? What a grand and intoxicating innocence. How could I be so naive? There is no deal.

Buying the $85.22 bundle--on sale 55% off, by the way, $190.68 normally, at the time of writing (it WILL go higher as they churn out more DLC)--does not actually include all of the DLC for this game. But even if you did then fill in all of the gaps by buying the rest of this game's DLC, you would still be missing content, because guess what? Want to play even the most basic, straightforward factions in Warhammer, like, I don't know, the Empire? Well sorry, your only option for the Empire is some totally-not-a-vampire-chick--the actual Emperor Karl Franz is off limits, along with most other factions in their ENTIRETY--unless you also buy BOTH of their previous Total War: Warhammer games, AND the DLC for those games in order to ACTUALY actually unlock everything. I think, anyway. Maybe their is some secret extra DLC I'll find out the game is withholding from me later on, they've done it once before already, after all.

The way they're chopping up their game so they can sell the parts of it that were already in the previous game back to you piecemeal nearly rivals EA in it's brazen greed--the only thing that keeps it from being on par being the fact that if you already owned the the previous games and their DLC, then I guess you don't need to rebuy it.

Also, just to be crystal clear, abhorrent DLC practices aside, even if I had gotten the game and all of its DLC for free (and all of the previous games and their DLC too, since again you need that for all this game's content), I still wouldn't recommend the game on the basis of quality either. At its best, it's just fine. But generally it's worse than Total War: SHOGUN 2, a game in same Total War series that came out over 2 decades earlier. The series doesn't just seem to have not made any progress in ten+ years, but has actually regressed significantly. This game has worse balance, worse gameplay, is bloated with ability micromanagement, uninteresting tech and skill trees, generally shallower settlement management, and dozens of missing features that have been inexplicably been removed, like the ability to manage the shape/closeness of units to adapt to archer fire or melee combat, cutscenes for hero actions, pre-battle speeches. They made recruitment worse, because units can't navigate the map without being in a lord's army, so when you call for recruitment from an army, instead of the unit being created and then walking on it's own to your army, the army is frozen for several turns while your reinforcements are bottlenecked through a faction wide shared, magic teleporting "global recruitment". Or you do what I do and have a scuffed system of "recruitment jockey" lords who's only job is ferrying back and forth units to your front line. Moving multiple units uniformly and precisely as a formation used to have a convenient button panel, but now it's hidden away behind hotkeys that you've got to look up every ten seconds. It also has gamebreaking bugs, like for example, when entering the realm of Chaos, the game might just softlock by having a cutscene never end, leaving you with letterboxing covering 50% of your screen, and no way to open the game's menu.

Years and years of work and the game feels stagnated. I just don't like where the game is at. A lot of focus has been given to huge issues like monetization and DLC structure. But even beyond that I just don't care for any of the campaign maps or how the world is presented. 

The Old World covered in chaos absolutely everywhere just isn't as interesting to me, there's no interesting conflicts between the Empire and Brettonia since both are busy fending off chaos attacks. It's all just so... boring.

I've gotten a lot of hours out of this game. But it seems doomed to be the worst of the three and the most dissapointing by far, no matter what the devs promise.

when 85% of your game is paid dlc to the point where not even the starting "free" factions - which are only a fraction of the total themselves - have access to all their units and mechanics is insane. It doesn't matter how good the game is, I will never recommend this game to anyone. Not in this economy.

Unfortunately whilst the game is good the company represents the very worst of capitalism. They feel entitled to your money and don't give a damn about providing value for it. All the dlcs are the equivalent of adding new rows in excel with different stats. They think they are being clever, but don't let them take advantage of you. We need to bring back a real meritocracy, where people at the top actually have spent a lifetime of training in the industry they are trying to run

you need to buy dlc or another game if your faction isnt the one they allowed you to play after you just paid the full game.
im pirating this game and every single one of its dlc.
go ♥♥♥♥ yourself

just about 500 hours on three alone, been playing for about a decade across the franchise. I love the fact that all my investments from one and two  over to three. Makes wanting to buy the next one when it comes out that much more, because i know all my characters will carry over. The game is fun and challenging and is always different. Nothing plays the exact same twice. Mods help add more flavor without changing the game, Its a great game and i want more like the little war hammer fiend I am.

The game is really amazing but i really dislike the monetisation of the game.
-unlike other games like stellaris. The dlc cant be shared over multiplayer campaigns. Its essentially double to triple dipping into savings. Its  a practise i really dislike and have avoided encouraging.
If you come late to the series , the game is a big investment for those who wanna try all the races.

-  gameplay wise , the game is very fun and enjoyable. with alot of factions to choose from (most you have to buy from previous games). Campaigns can take 100-300 turns so you are in for the long haul with faction gameplay. each faction has an interesting mechanic for example : cathay (ying and yang ), chaos dwarves (money, resources, minerals and seats of power that give buffs), empire (confederating the empire together to fight against chaos with alot of politicing.)

-units are really varied and fun. this usually synchs with a faction theme as well.

Not as good as Warhammer II
The game adds many new factions but doesn't really change much in terms of mechanics, and unlocking new factions requires a lot of purchases
Worth buying during a sale

It's an okay game. 

PROS:
UI is great and easy to read
Lots of choice (maybe too much ;p) of people to be.
Combat is alright.

CONS:
Loading times are too long (about 1-2 minutes) and throw me off the game.
Siege battles are un-fun and hard to look at (i auto resolve).

Personally wouldn't recommend as whilst the negatives don't seem that much, they hit hard.
The massive anti player bias the AI has is ridiculous. Do anything and the 3 most powerful factions around you declare ware. I am playing on normal difficultly. F this.
$300+ for all the content in the game is totally ridiculous. Genuinely one of the most greedy companies I've come across, and I am a regular pdx interactive player.

Just like the older Warhammer games, this one is an absolute blast. The graphics look fantastic and the actual gameplay is solid. There is something so satisfying about building up your empire from scratch and juggling your economy, public order, and armies. Plus, nothing beats the feeling of completely wiping out some random enemy that had the nerve to declare war on you out of nowhere. The battles can take a minute to get used to, but they are incredibly fun once it clicks. Overall, it’s a great game and I’d highly recommend grabbing it if you’re into strategy stuff.

Many, many bugs!!!!!  Why haven't they been fixed?

Warhammer III is the type of game that is right up my alley.  While frustrating to learn, it has the complexity and variety in the gameplay that I appreciate.  

However, the game has several annoying bugs that ruin game play...  constant crashes, strange things happening mid-game, and now it has completely stopped working and the game won't even load anymore.  The tutorial campaign can't be completed. Continuing my campaign brings me to a save from 100 turns ago, before crashing again!!!   etc, etc, etc...  

All of my problems are all over the internet so it's definitely not just me, yet no one cares enough to fix any of them.  Such massive potential turned into complete junk of a game... a completely frustrating bad joke!!!

I've played 500+ hours of WH2 and 175+ hours of WH3. The campaign has become too congested, too big, and with too many mechanics, too many new factions (and more to come). WH1 factions (BRETONNIA) still haven't been brough up to WH3 standards. It feels like quantity over quality.

Sieges just suck in this game whether attacking or defending. I actively auto-resovle even if it means losing units because it is that much of a grind.

The QOL and UI is better than WH2 but that's about all that is an improvement.

The game is still fun for the most part, but feels like a step backwards since WH2 overall.

the game is complex with out being strategic.   I't not about using the mechanics in the game for an outcome its more just hanging on longer than the Ai which turns a corner at some point and you start to win.  the worst thing you can do is actually try to win the game. 
If the answer is mostly:

survive the opening script
exploit AI weaknesses
wait for the snowball
The strategic depth is shallower than it appears.

the propsed strategice mechanics feel less like a strategic challenge and more like a scoreboard tracking things that happen around you.
For example:

A diplomacy system where nobody ever accepts deals.
A trade system that contributes almost nothing.
A loyalty system that never actually affects behavior.
The mechanic might as well not exist.

I bought this because of the 75% off sale, and it seems pretty much unplayable. Despite running through any community fix I could find it still reliably crashes to desktop every few turns. This problem seems to be getting worse with each entry in the series and I doubt I'll be giving CA any money in the future because of it. It's a real shame because this kind of game is my cup of tea.

Game was fine, I would need way more time to trully comment on how the game really plays. Reason i give negative review is the fact that you need bunch of DLCs AND Warhammer 1 and 2 to unlock all the playable factions. Insane cash grab, which i simply refuse to pay for and in my opinion all of this should be part of the base game. 

Note that im fairly rich and this wouldn't be that big of a hit on my bank account and still i refuse to pay out of principle and for the greed im refunding. 

Like honestly, why would i buy full game, which i wouldnt even play, to play few factions in this game? Mental IMHO

Do not buy this game!!!!
The game is unplayable and it does not launch at the moment
Quite possibly one of the worst gaming experiences I've had the misfortune of subjecting myself to in recent years. That's saying a LOT.

I've played a few hundred hours of Total Warhammer across the three iterations, and I want to start by saying that there is entertainment value to be had here...if you're willing to pay the enormous cost of entry and do the work necessary to make up for the shortcomings of the game's lackluster, decade-old engine. At this point, SEGA and CA have long since abandoned spending development time on making meaningful improvements to the game. They're just churning out new Legendary Lords at $7.99 each. They've been doing this for quite some time, and by now the game feels pretty bloated and ramshackle.

If you want to be able to play with all of the various factions and lords that are available after ten years, you're going to need to shell out hundreds of dollars. Even on sale. After doing that, you'll have purchased a game that is really starting to show its age. It's not well optimized. The AI is a complete joke to play against. There are still some frustrating bugs lurking around, and it's very unlikely that any of this will be fixed any time soon. 

The game looks incredible on decent hardware, and the battles are a joy to engage with for any fan of the Warhammer universe or the Total War franchise. The tactical level is where this game really shines, but the strategic/campaign layer is almost painful to work through at this stage. There are mods on the Workshop that you can get to mitigate some of the problems with this game, but honestly, trying to cobble together the right combination of them feels like trying to plug holes in a sinking ship. 

Total Warhammer 40K is right around the corner, with a brand new engine that looks absolutely fantastic. If I were you, I'd save your money and buy that when it launches. I'm sure that they will monetize the hell out of it, just like they do with this, but at least it'll be built from the ground up to work on modern hardware and incorporate everything they learned making this.

bought the game to play as skaven however, you cannot play as skaven unless you buy a different game or dlc from a different game. what's the point? i understand dlc as a concept but theres not even a simple way to get dlc like in every other well made game that puts a 10-50 dollar price tag to unlock absolutely everything instead you have to buy what you want individually or pay hundreds of dollars for the full experience. Game has been sitting in my backlog too long for me to refund so now i have a game that doesn't do anything i bought it for.
Overloaded with DLC options for pretty much 60% of factions that could be played in the game.
you totally f***ed this game after the updates. it can't no longer be payed. it crashes when launching all the time.

I picked it up while it was on a massive sale, and even then to unlock most (not even all) content it was ~130 bucks. I think off sale it's ~330. I played at and I enjoyed it, but I asked myself: will I enjoy this more than the 10 or more other games I could pick up on sale for the same price? nah, so I refunded it. The dlc of this is outrageous.

ridiculous amounts of DLC, even buying the collection bundle keeps like half the factions locked
they want you to buy all 3 warhammer games for certain DLCs. the pack that included them all is normally $170

a Bloat of DLCs. base game without them:

3 Campaigns: 
-1) the tutorial. a tutorial so invasive to level of gacha games click this close this window move this unit but i dont let you move it because i move your camera every 2 seconds.
-2) the other campaign. i dont know and i dont care at this point.
-3) the campaign that requieres you to buy (total warhammer 1) to play it.

so this game dont have 16 dlcs it has 34 counting the warhammer 1 and 2.

after playing old total war games this feel like an insult and entirely kill my hype on 40k too...
I haven't picked up a TW game since Empire. Real strategy and a lot of fun storytelling!

Suddenly "own" this game, but its actually an expansion of Immortel Empires for Part 1 and 2, which is nice to have for free i guess.
but now it says that I already own Part 3 warning me not to buy it again? so confused...
Also having so many factions as paid DLC is really bad

The game is only decent with mods and every patch and update breaks them and then it takes weeks for things to sort out. The draw out pace of DLC schedule is making the game near unplayable at times. Most of the great Modders are abandoning the game, and CA is too arrogant to competently adopt key issues modders fixed ages ago.

Revision after 17 more hours: Still sucks.

The econ, AI, build times, load screens, end turn sequence, it's all terrible. This game is BAD, a slog that feels like I'm in 2013. The campaign is bad, most of it just requires you to follow a script.

This isn't a game that's subjectively bad, it's objectively bad, and the pricing alone should really clue people in.

Somehow the game with over a decades worth of DLC spanning three titles all conglomerating into a giant buggy mess that equates to hundreds of dollars is the more affordable and approachable game when compared to it's tabletop cousin.

I used to be able to recommend this game. I loved 2, and while 3 had problems, they eventually added a supermap version of 2 into this.

But lately, they've just been adding hero after hero, with a price tag for each. This isn't a $100 game. I'd pay $30.

The game is very enjoyable, and one of the best experiences for warhammer fantasy games I ever had. But Creative Assembly's horrible DLC policy makes this probably the worst total war game to get into by far. Unless you are prepared to spend over 300 pounds, you'll have maybe a quarter of factions to choose from. 

The game runs surprisingly fine for me (on a macbook pro), but has some serious texture overlay issues. I have not experienced any other major technical issues aside from very few crashes. 
The game has the Kingdoms of Ind and the Hinterlands of Khuresh present on the map but even with the addition of Bhashiva I highly doubt these two regions would be receiving DLC. Who knows? Maybe they'll add them in an overpriced racepack in five years time. 

If you adore warhammer fantasy, you'll enjoy the game. If you would like to get into warhammer fantasy, don't do it through this game. Though it does prepare you for the ridiculous pricing of the tabletop. And with the upcoming release of total war wh40k, I trust that the combined prices of dlc in that game will overshadow whatever CA did with this game.
You can't play the open campaign map without purchasing factions through DLC. This is not a TOTAL WAR game.

You pay $60 for 20% of a game, then you're expected to pay $200 for the rest of the game (DLC), game is a horribly unplayable mess, dont even think about playing with your friend either unless BOTH of you have the 10$ DLC that lets you play on the map that isnt 300 factions (takes 3-4 minutes for ONE TURN TO COMPLETE)

it's a 10/10 of game, with 0/10 bugs
I regret paying that much money for all three games + every DLC and not being able to play the game without a mandatory crash, that breaks the entire campaign on a random turn.
Just waiting for the finished version of the game. I'm tired of the constant updates breaking my campaigns and mods.

Worst new player experience I've ever had.

You get acces to like 5 factions, with their rosters heavily limited some of which to the point of being basically unusable (like the tzench) since they are missing core units. The game is also programmed to just piss you off with "anti-player bias" and it feels like if you don't look up and follow an exact guide on how to play the first 10-20 turns you will just get obliterated. Make the mistake of playing on easy? The game is going to straight up lie to you about battles and make  you feel like a complete worthless idiot when you can't win a battle it claims is a decisive victory with low losses. I spent nearly 100 hours trying to learn brettonians (Simply because they are the only faction that doesnt have half their roster taken away from them by default) and despite 10+ additional hours looking at guides and trying to follow them exactly. I just can't reproduce anything. The AI does not behave like it does in those videos. This game is probably really fun, and probably quit easy, to people who have been playing these games for over a decade but trying to play anything other than grand cathay as a fresh player with no DLCs or previous titles was an absolute nightmare and worst gaming experiences I've ever had.

This game isn't even worth the $8 I paid for it, let alone the over $300+ they want just to unlock everything in the game.
0/5 stars, the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game keeps crashing at the tutorial lmao. Pure garbage

You have to pay way too much money to get the full experience for this game. The scummiest dev team that I have ever seen. Without getting the DLCs and the other 2 Warhammer titles you are missing 75% of the game. Disgusting business practices.

Since the last patch the game crashes whenever you try to save. I'm not alone facing this. Lost hours of hard fought battles due to this bug. 
This is unacceptable and negative reviews are the only way to have CA fix the game..

This game is mid at best compared to other Total War games, but the mods make it great but then the devs make it garbage. The devs roll out a major update and it breaks your campaign, okay whatever thats fine. But then they don't properly test the patch and roll out 2-3 more hotfixes for some stupid sht they missed like a ui fix that then breaks your modded campaign again and again. Why are these devs treating this game like its live service?? Every new dlc gets sht reviews because these devs keep rolling things out prematurely and breaking everything several times per patch. I've spent 1000 hours and I can only say i've had one actual satisfying 100+ turn campaign because the campaign breaks every single patch.

I want to love this game. I love the setting and the vibe. I have played it a lot. But I just can not recommend it.
I understand that sometimes it takes time to fix bugs, especially AI related. But this has been going on for years and years and there are still soo many issues that just suck the fun out of the game. And instead of fixing problems that plague this game for literally years, they publish DLC after DLC and anounce that they will fix certain issues after the DLCs. It is not fun anymore and I do not see any improvement, especially because they have promised stuff like that in the past and nothing happened. 
Can not recommend to buy. Sadly.

I have a list of complaints:
-All your favorite table-top units are missing their normal spectacle and character thanks to CA not taking the time to include unit command groups (champions, standard bearers, etc.).
-The vanilla climate and corruption designs are entirely defanged to the point where it may as well not even exist, in favor of giving people and easy game.
-A lack of unit type limits means you will routinely see absurdly stupid stuff like an army of 15 catapults and 1 hero.

But mods make the game playable and I'll give them a thumbs up for CA getting half-way to the goal.

It's a great game, I'm downvoting because I paid through the prologue only to find that the main story campaign- not what most people are coming here for, but still the supposed core of the game, is a DLC.

Poorly designed AI constantly cheat (as in they do things that are literally impossible for you to do when you control the same army). Yes there are ways round it. but if you have to basically use exploits and cheese with a lot of the factions instead of actual tactics just to play the game (and research all the cheesy meta instead of actually being a better strategist), that means the developer has dropped the ball. I still like playing the game to an extent, I just hate the "one rule for me and another for thee" band aid that gets slapped over the AI to cover the fact that they are poorly designed. I don't like being forced to use meta cheese instead of actual strategy, it makes the game substantially less fun.

this is a amazing game the daemon prince customization needs to be reworked so you dont have to use body parts all for one god and some units need to be reskinned but all in all its a great game totally worth the money.

Constant crashes after every update and thousands of people reporting the same issues year after year. I've tried every single fix that comes up when the issues are reported and nothing sticks. At best you load an earlier save and get a few turns further along before the issues start up again.
The game has poor balance. It's frustrating. The load times are atrocious. Half of the content is DLC. This game makes it seem as if the point of playing is to waste your time.

This game has all of the same ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ mechanics that the first 2 games have, that modders are always burdened to fix, MODDERS ALWAYS HAVE TO FIX YOUR ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ GAME. 
And on top of that, a bunch of cash grab dlc crap, i cant even play all the races i liked from the last games.
LAME.
Don't buy this game unless you get it near free and check the races you can play, because it a good chance they wont have them free or even in dlc. 
LAME!
They only single thing, i mean THE ONLY single thing better about this game is the new map has slightly fast turn speed.

Damn ! I can't play as Empire because I don't have the first game !

So sad, because the game is nice.

Guess I'll play Bretonnia.

Pure trash garbage.  When fighting battles 1/2 the time the AI REFUSES TO ATTACK FOR NO REASON.  Just got done losing a battle as I watch my archer line just jack into a can for all the good they are doing.  No attacking, just standing there.  And this is a known issue since WH1 it looks like.  Avoid spending any more money on this dev until they learn how to program and not just copy/paste from others and expect money.

DO NOT BUY. I am a long time fan of Total War going way back, some of my favorite games. That has been completely ruined by this company. This is the second time in a row where they punish you for not buying the DLC. When I mean punish I mean they give the AI all the top-tier units but you do not have access to the necessary units to counter them. If you like lizardmen, you need to buy 3 DLCs to have their full roster! 

You buy the game just to see you have 3 factions unlocked, the rest are all greyed out, you go to one of the few factions you have access to and see that half the lords are greyed out, and then you start playing one you do have access to, only to learn a few hours in that you don't have access to the top half of the unit tree. 

This is wrong and they know it. Do not buy this game unless its 10 years from now and you can get the full bundle for $10.

Even if I only have 27 hours at this time of making this recommendation, I can calmly say this game is an masterpiece. It is even more fun with more legendary Lords/hero's from the DLC, along with previous titles. An solid 10/10 in my book.

how is it in playing any other total war title i end up losing less men, and winning more battles fighting them manually rather than autoresolving it. but whenever i play any fantasy warhammer title, it's the complete opposite. the empire's infantry units lose to similar units in melee combat. artillery is next to useless. even the greatswords in empire service will rout despite having over half of their unit health still present, despite being in combat with units with similar stats. 
and, to add insult to injury, my keyboard and mouse keeps unattaching itself from my PC whenever i play this game.
This is honestly one of my favorite games ever. it is not without out its flaws but its a damn good time
Well, i don"t recommend it, because after only 1,683hrs, i grew sick of it. This is way too little, unacceptable!
the only thing that saves this game is the modding community.  Its been out for years and very basic issues with AI and unit controls still exist.
After spending 100 hours fighting save corruption and crashes in multiplayer instead of Chaos, I’ve learned that the most devastating spell in the game is apparently "Change Language Settings".
takes a while to under stand the game but when you do it's really fun to see your armies clash head on even more if you have a singly entity unit like a great unclean one
game feels smooth and easy for me to learn. been waiting a long time to play this and worth it for sure. i should probly say more but i got nothing else to say.

Warhammer world is based on real world history,cultures and mythology. World is kinda looking like real world map.
It is very good game at some point of view. Bcs. like every total war game base game is like only chassis of the car. If you want full experience and fun,you need to buy other parts (dlcs).
They charge 5€ for packs only with reskin of figures. And full game is like 450€. Disgusting company.

For strategy games fans it is must have,but play first base game and don´t buy dlc´s or buy just what you want to play.

Total War: Warhammer III is one of the best fantasy strategy games available. The combination of massive real-time battles, unique factions, and a huge campaign map offers hundreds of hours of gameplay.
Started out rough, but with IE and getting some DLC on sale this is as good as it gets for warhammer fantasy.
Perfect game in many aspects but the long victory conditions are straight up torture for some races
Obviously the best one out there in the warhammer series, since it not only unites its predecessors, but also offers much more possibilities, a better graphic and so much more....

Been playing total war games since shogun 2 and i can say this game is top tier. Alot of dlc so just buy only what you want to play or if on sale. So much to say about this but too lazy atm to actually lay out. Comment any game questions and i'll try to answer to the best of my ability.

I really enjoy a lot of aspects of this game, but the battles sometimes are ridiculous, i dont know how many times i have to reroute my people from chasing retreating armies to the edge of the map, or sometimes my troops will randomly attack people and sometimes they wont, the ai needs to be rewritten completely

I lowkey think its pretty good with mods but still decent as the base game and ima be honest if you like me and a lil bum who is ♥♥♥♥ at strategy it ain't worth it but if you are willing to learn this simple game then you pretty good tbh
You either win the fight and chase the fleeing until you kill every single one of them or relpay the battle so you can finish off everybody
Tests your strategy to the max, lot of re-playability, some good backing music and comical goblin character voices, excelent graphics and game play

Finish download the game.
start playing as Boris Ursus as I like bear

Then 'The Everchosen' declare war on me.

For all of its problems, this game is uniquely adiciting and fantastic. Watching masive armies clashing, preparing your last stand on the field of battle, it's a great feeling. If you like strategy, fantasy and masive armies I highly recommend it.

It's a fun game. I've played about 500 hours of this series incl Warhammer 2. Does it have its flaws? Yes, plenty. But, overall does it deliver a unique, fun war-game? Yes, absolutely. The only thing to consider is that this is a paid games as a service game. You kinda need to buy three games and like 10 different DLCs to get the whole experience. You should wait for a sale (they happen often enough) and scoop up some of the DLCs that interest you.

One of the best strategy games I've ever played! BUT the constant forced updates via Steam wreck your campaign and especially lately, require extreme workarounds on a technical level. Otherwise, you just lose hours of play. It's great that the content keeps coming, but I want to have the option to update or not if I'm in the middle of a long play! Sort it out CA...

After almost a thousand hours playing Warhammer3, (this is not including the time in 1 and 2), I thought I would throw a positive review in. 
You have to like playing Total War games. If you haven't played RTS games like this before, try it, but be prepared to study the game like learning chess. This is what I like about it!
Hundreds of unique units, and characters, and damage types affected by scenario and environment... and that's just the bit of the complexity. A lot to understand to have a positive experience. Amazing.
Definitely room to improve with the AI, both the campaign and battle decision making. There some things that don't work as described, and some things that have been bugged since launch. Not perfect, but if you like Warhammer lore, like real-time battles, and have the patience of a saint to wait on the 250 factions to take their turns... this game is awesome!
Overall, it's okay. If there is a neutral option, I would choose neutral in this game. But there isn't, so I recommend.
Real Time Strategic Battles in the Warhammer Universe. 4x Very Fun

Its is a beautiful game design with really cool lore details. I love how engaging the combat is and I really like the diplomacy especially with factions like Bretonnia is top tier. However unlike other games like Total War Rome 2 and Total War Dynasties the city building is less engaging. Despite this I believe this game is a top 10 GOAT.

If you want to play with all the units of any race, you have to buy the base game (which includes races you might not want to play as), an army DLC (unless it's part of the base game), and several lord packs (that also include content for races you might not want to play as, and would have to buy the base army for anyway). You have to do that to access units and lords that exist in the base game, and the AI factions will use, but are put behind a paywall for the player - just to tempt you into buying them. You then have in-game advertising for the DLC...and annoying little flame icons promoting the latest carved out chunk of the game in the start menus. 
 I'm not sure how much worse it could get without them adding loot-box gambling, or cash-for-cosmetics, to the mix.

It's annoying because the actual game is quite good. I just don't want to give them any more money for that sort of foulness.
Super fun game. Tons of features, countless hours of content.

It is a great TOTAL WAR. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Se amate la strategia profonda, la gestione millimetrica e le battaglie campali epiche, questo titolo è un acquisto obbligato. Attualmente è l'apice della serie: credo sinceramente che solo un futuro Medieval Total War 3 potrà fare di meglio.

⏳ Longevità: 95/100 – Monumentale. La presenza di così tante fazioni uniche e l'accesso a ben due campagne completamente differenti (con obiettivi e mappe diverse) garantisce una rigiocabilità praticamente infinita. Superare il turno 100 è solo l'inizio.

🎮 Gameplay: 90/100 – Solido, appagante e strategico. Le battaglie richiedono vera tattica e la gestione dell'economia sulla mappa è profonda. La formula Total War qui esprime il suo massimo potenziale.

🎨 Grafica: 90/100 – Tutto funziona a dovere. Il livello di dettaglio dei modelli, gli effetti sul campo e la pulizia visiva generale regalano un impatto visivo eccellente in ogni scontro.

🧠 Curva di Apprendimento (Learning Curve): 60/100 – Non è un gioco immediato. Per iniziare a padroneggiare davvero le meccaniche, l'economia delle province e i punti di forza di ogni fazione, mettete in conto di doverci giocare almeno 50 ore. Ma ogni ora investita viene ampiamente ripagata.

🛡️ Stabilità: Bugs Free – L'esperienza di gioco è solida e pulita. Il codice è stabile, permettendoti di goderti lunghe sessioni di gioco senza la frustrazione di crash improvvisi o bug che rompono la campagna.

Voto Complessivo: Assolutamente consigliato.
It's not very well optimized, but I still keep coming back, and can't not recommend it in the end
Love the game except all the DLCs but the steam mods and custom stuff is really good!

Incredible game. It stands out from a lot of other total war games with scale and army balance. A lot of the factions are genuinely unique, unlike, for example, Medieval 2 which, while a great game and my first total war, lacked that variety. The modding community is great as well, giving the game almost infinite replayability
Fun game, the rift spawn timer in (ROC) need adjustment, some faction are ok with it and other suffer from it.

My review is very specifically Co-op multi player related, it's a fun game and ive enjoyed my near 300 hours in this game. If you mainly are playing this co-op with your friends with mods that you enjoy for your playstyle then it's totally worth it. I wont speak to the single play experience as ive never done that
It's Total War but with big creatures, magic and lots of fun. Definitely has its issues, but I keep coming back and having fun with the game.

Pretty solid, Read the reviews for the DLC's and only buy the ones with races you actually play.

Don't buy this on anything less than 50% off.

Game been running 24/7 on my 2nd monitor, really nice turn based strategy+factions+DLCS - i don't mind how long it takes, sure there is mods to reduce time/main objectives but its really something you can enjoy idle or serious gameplay like an investment - the game does a good job of babysitting you through the complex mechanics like diplomacy/trading/making allies or enemies

the game is like solid 8 or 9/10  but with mods 10/10 - you need life quality mods that fixes some hiccups - yes the story campaign is short and feels like tutorial but each battle i played even on eternal campaign got some story for each faction like their endgame goal, i love the game and plan on playing it constantly.

knights? dragons? guns? helicopters? magic? dinosaurs? 
If you have ever imagined playing with all of the above in a huge sandbox, this game might just scratch that itch.

Total war 

Warhammer 1 was great, because it is the first fantasy title and such an attractive IP at that
Warhammer 2 was meh with out mods and amazing with mods
Warhammer 3 is amazing out of the box even without mods

Turn order is fast, saved replay can be watched or refought. all the races have extensive and unique mechanics. Ability to disable AI stats cheats in options, enemy AI blob into huge 4+ stack armies so you get to do epic fights. selectable end game crisis that makes game hard again just when you are getting too strong.

Been a big fan of the Total war franchise but never liked the idea of the fantasy element in Warhammer.. boy was I wrong to dismiss! The amount of fun I'm having playing this game has made me go out and invest in a new computer. Enough said

*Caveat* I recommend this only to warhammer fans or people who have friends who play. This game has crazy dlc farming and its an ugly practice but it is incredibly fun. So wait for a warhammer skulls event sale and pick it up then!

all three warhammers Immortal Empires is the greatest when u play all 3WH with all DLCs but the makers give all the Wrong Factions and races the last FEW DLC Tzeentch still only has two factions wtf no free legendary lord... the choices for dlc are totally BIAS but im only bothered by that because the game still the best
Slaughtering wood elf men, women, children and making Skolden into a submissive little dog is what brings me joy in life.

Total War: Warhammer III è un ottimo strategico, divertente e molto profondo. Mi è piaciuta soprattutto la varietà delle fazioni e il fatto che ogni partita richieda un po' di pianificazione e strategia. Giocarlo con gli amici rende tutto ancora più bello, soprattutto nelle campagne cooperative.

L'unico piccolo difetto è che, come molti giochi del genere, la campagna a volte può risultare un po' lenta. Nel complesso però è un gioco che consiglio assolutamente agli amanti della strategia.
